title: narcissus elegans subsp. fallax
intro: Narcissus elegans subsp. fallax, also known as the fall-flowering daffodil, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the genus Narcissus and is characterized by its trumpet-shaped flowers and slender leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Narcissus elegans subsp. fallax include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner.
